title: Xi Is Poised to Press Trump on Arms Sales to Taiwan
summary: Beijing has called Taiwan the “core of China’s core interests.” Xi Jinping is likely to focus on getting President Trump to slow approval of more weapons for the self-governing island.

title: China Seeks A.I. Independence, Weakening Trump’s Leverage
summary: Before this week’s U.S.-Chinese summit, Beijing reached a milestone in its quest for technological self-sufficiency.

title: China Increasingly Views Trump’s America as an Empire in Decline
summary: For decades, many Chinese viewed the United States with a mix of admiration, envy and resentment. President Trump’s volatile second term shattered that image.

title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.

title: Supreme Court Clears Path for Alabama to Use New Voting Map
summary: A majority of the justices sided with Alabama in a move that could speed up efforts to put in place a congressional district map that would eliminate a majority-Black district.

title: The Voting Rights Decision Might Silence Black People in Fayette County, Tennessee
summary: Black residents, who make up 25 percent of Fayette County, Tenn., won a new electoral map last year to change an all-white board of commissioners. They worry the Supreme Court’s ruling could threaten that.

title: Virginia Officials Ask Supreme Court to Restore Voting Map Drawn by Democrats
summary: State officials asked the justices to overturn a Virginia Supreme Court decision that struck down a congressional map, a major defeat for Democrats.

title: Why Republicans Are Still Drawing House Maps, While Democrats Are Stuck
summary: The G.O.P. has seized an edge on redistricting thanks to two court rulings, and it has more room to maneuver for extra seats before the midterms.

title: Trump Nominates Cameron Hamilton to Lead FEMA
summary: President Trump chose Cameron Hamilton to direct federal disaster response. As acting head of FEMA last year, he had opposed abolishing the agency and was ousted.

title: Justice Dept. Subpoenas Wall Street Journal in Leak Investigation
summary: Critics raised concerns over press freedom after The Journal disclosed receiving subpoenas related to an article on deliberations on the risks of military action in Iran.

title: Reflecting Pool Repairs to Cost $13.1 Million. Trump Had Promised $1.8 Million.
summary: The Interior Department added $6.2 million to the no-bid contract for repairs to the Lincoln Memorial pool late last week.

title: Kari Lake, Who Led Effort to Shutter Voice of America, Is Named Ambassador to Jamaica
summary: Ms. Lake tried and failed to fire hundreds of Voice of America journalists and to cut funding for other federally funded news groups like Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty.

title: Nations Brace for Long-Term Economic Woes as Trump Calls Iran Truce Plan ‘Garbage’
summary: President Trump said the fragile cease-fire was on “life support,” and India’s leader urged residents to cut back on purchases and travel.

title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
summary: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.

title: Is France’s Centrist Leader Trying to Weatherproof It From the Far Right?
summary: President Emmanuel Macron has appointed a string of allies who will remain in critical state positions after next year’s presidential election, and who could thwart a far-right successor.

title: Gisèle Pelicot’s Memoir Said Something Original and Taboo About Victimhood
summary: A book by the world’s most famous survivor of sexual violence has been read as a manifesto or a cry of pain. What she wrote is far more complicated.

title: Israel Prepares to Prosecute First Suspects in Oct. 7 Attack Led by Hamas
summary: Parliament passed a new law that paves the way for military trials for hundreds of Palestinians suspected of taking part in the 2023 attack that ignited the two-year war in Gaza.

title: Former Hostage Calls on All of Israel’s Government to Resign
summary: Rom Braslavski, who said he had endured starvation, torture and sexual abuse during two-year captivity, demanded that members “take responsibility and get out of our lives.”

title: Altar Boy Who Called Out Sexual Abuse in the Philippines Is Now a Lawyer for Victims
summary: Michal Gatchalian dared to speak out against sexual abuse by a priest in the devoutly Catholic Philippines. He’s now a lawyer helping other victims.

title: Putin Said the War ‘Is Coming to a Close,’ but That’s Not All He Said
summary: The Russian leader is walking a tightrope: acknowledging public discontent while offering no indication that he might give up on his demands.

title: Ukraine Looks to Step Away From Trump and U.S.
summary: With peace talks on ice and Ukraine now more self-reliant, President Volodymyr Zelensky seems to be stepping away from the United States.

title: Former Top Zelensky Aide Is Accused in Corruption Inquiry
summary: Ukrainian news outlets named the suspect as Andriy Yermak, who resigned last year amid a sweeping scandal.

title: Quebec Novelist Shines a Harsh Spotlight on Migration, and on Trump’s America
summary: Thélyson Orélien, a Haitian-born, previously unknown writer, submitted an unsolicited manuscript. Its rights were sold in 23 countries before publication.

title: Pedro Sánchez Is Donald Trump’s Nemesis, and He’s Winning
summary: Pedro Sánchez has established an alternative governing philosophy to Trumpism.

title: Democrats Can’t Let This Antisemitic Sex Therapist Win Her Runoff
summary: Maureen Galindo’s rise demonstrates how even justified rage and despair can curdle into hateful paranoia.

title: Stephen Colbert Hosts 4 of His ‘Best Television Friends’
summary: Jimmy Kimmel, Jimmy Fallon, John Oliver and Seth Meyers, co-hosts of the Strike Force Five podcast along with Colbert, joined as guests on what is likely their last appearance together on the “Late Show.”

title: Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang Will Plead Guilty to Working as Chinese Agent
summary: Eileen Wang, who resigned Monday as the mayor of Arcadia, Calif., published propaganda on a purported news site under direction from Chinese officials, prosecutors said.

title: Instructure Strikes Deal for Hackers for Return of Canvas Data
summary: Instructure, which provides Canvas software to thousands of schools and universities around the world, did not say what it had given the hackers in exchange for the stolen data.
